super-mario-bros-2.title: Super Mario Bros. 2

facts.label: Designers
facts.value: Kensuke Tanabe, Yasuhisa Yamamura, Hideki Konno

facts.label: Programmers
facts.value: Toshihiko Nakago, Yasunori Taketani, Toshio Iwawaki

facts.label: NA Release
facts.value: October 9, 1988 (JP origin as Yume Kojo: Doki Doki Panic, July 10, 1987)

facts.label: Playable Characters
facts.value: Mario, Luigi, Toad, Princess Toadstool (each with distinct abilities)

facts.label: Signature Mechanic
facts.value: Pick up and throw enemies & pulled vegetables

story: Nintendo of America rejected the Japanese Super Mario Bros. 2 as too difficult and too similar to the original, so instead of releasing it in the West they reskinned Yume Kojo: Doki Doki Panic — a 1987 Fuji Television advergame designed by Kensuke Tanabe — with Mario, Luigi, Toad, and Princess Toadstool.

Its trademark lift-and-throw mechanic and a cast of new enemies (Shy Guy, Bob-omb, Birdo, and Pokey) became permanent staples of the Mario franchise. The game later received a 16-bit remake in Super Mario All-Stars (1993) and remains one of the best-selling NES titles of all time.
